quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592. He claimed to have discovered a passage between the Pacific and Atlantic oceans, though historians still debate whether he actually sailed these waters. The strait is home to orcas, gray whales, and over 200 species of birds!

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels can fly backwards, upside down, and their hearts beat up to 1,260 times per minute! Discover the amazing abilities of hummingbirds and why they're nature's helicopters.

medium_read.title: Hungary's Thermal Bath Culture
medium_read.teaser: Budapest has more thermal springs than any other capital city in the world, and Hungarians have been enjoying healing baths for over 2,000 years. This beautiful tradition combines Roman engineering, Turkish architecture, and Hungarian hospitality into a unique cultural treasure.

long_read.title: The Olympic Peninsula: Where Wilderness Meets the Sea
long_read.teaser: From temperate rainforests to rugged coastlines, the Olympic Peninsula is one of the most diverse ecosystems in North America. Learn about the unique plants, animals, and landscapes that make your home region so extraordinary.

port_angeles.title: Black-tailed Deer in Olympic National Park
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Columbian black-tailed deer with their adorable spotted fawns around Port Angeles and Hurricane Ridge. These gentle deer are smaller than their eastern cousins and are unique to the Pacific Northwest. Early morning or dusk are the best times to see them grazing in meadows.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's wings can beat up to ___ times per second during courtship displays.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! Those tiny wings move so fast they create that distinctive humming sound we love!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! It's about 80 times per second — faster than our eyes can see!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: It's actually about 80 times per second! That's why they make that wonderful humming sound.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: During normal flight, hummingbird wings beat about 50 times per second, but males can increase this to 80 beats per second during their spectacular diving courtship displays to impress females.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Gyönyörű! That's right — pillangó is butterfly! Such a lovely, melodic word.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is pillangó! Virág means flower, madár means bird, and csillag means star — all beautiful words!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' comes from an older form meaning 'fluttering one.' Hungary is home to over 180 species of butterflies, and butterfly watching is a popular springtime activity in the countryside.

puzzle_3.question: What special ability do orcas in the Salish Sea use to navigate and hunt?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Yes, orcas use echolocation, clicking sounds that bounce back to create a 'sound picture' of their world. The Southern Resident orcas you might see from Port Angeles are masters at this!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's echolocation! Orcas send out clicking sounds that bounce back, helping them find salmon and navigate the waters of the Strait of Juan de Fuca even in darkness or murky water.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Orcas produce clicks that travel through the water and bounce off objects, returning as echoes that paint a detailed acoustic picture. Each orca pod has its own distinct dialect of calls, passed down through generations like a family language.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
